content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

February 8, 1984


David Nawi
General Counsel
Air Resources Board
P.O. Box 2815
Sacramento, CA  95812

Re:  Advice Letter No. A-84-013

Dear Mr. Nawi:

     Thank you for your request for advice on the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.  The facts, as
stated in your letter, are that the Motor Vehicle Manufacturers
Association ("MVMA") is a trade association consisting of
companies or their affiliates which manufacture motor vehicles.
The members are: American Motors Corporation, Chrysler,
M.A.N. Truck and Bus Company, PACCAR, Inc., Volkswagen of
America, Volvo of North America, Ford Motor Company, General
Motors and International Harvester.  The purpose of the MVMA is
to further the interests of its members.

     MVMA has suggested that it invite one or more of the Air
Resources Board members and staff to an informational meeting in
Detroit.  The MVMA will pay all of the expenses, including
transportation, lodging and meals.  The expenses will be paid
from the MVMA general fund, rather than from a special
assessment on some or all of its members.  The value of the
items received will exceed $250 for each person making the trip.

     The Air Resources Board makes various regulatory decisions
which affect the financial interests of motor vehicle
manufacturers.1/  It is unlikely that the Board will make any


1/  For example, the Board makes decisions concerning
vehicular emission standards and procedures which result in the
expenditure of substantial sums of money by affected
manufacturers.

David Nawi
February 8, 1984
Page 2


decisions that will affect the MVMA. Your question is, if the
MVMA provides certain Board members and staff with the trip to
Detroit, who will be considered to be the source of this gift
for the purposes of the Act's disclosure and disqualification
requirements.

DISCUSSION

Government Code Section 871002/ prohibits a public
official from making, or participating in the making of, a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest. An official has a "financial
interest" in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect on:

* * *

(c) Any source of income . . . aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to, received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is
made.

(Section 87103(c).)

Each person who accepts the trip to Detroit will be
receiving a gift worth more than $250. Gifts are considered to
be "income" under the Act. (Section 82030(a).) Under the
staff's current policy, the source of this income will be
considered to be MVMA, not the individual members of MVMA. This
policy developed because, in the past, the factual situations
that the staff has been confronted with have always supported
treating associations as separate from their members. At this
time, the staff feels obligated to continue to follow this
policy. However, the staff feels that, in a situation such as
you have presented, where an association has only a few members
who may be materially affected by governmental decision making,
there may be justification for treating both the association and
the members as sources of income. The staff plans to explore
the possibility of a regulation on this question, and we will
contact you if decide to propose a regulation on this topic.


2/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code.

David Nawi
February 8, 1984
Page 3


     Each Board and staff member receiving the trip must report
his or her share of the gift from the MVMA on his or her
Statement of Economic Interests.

     If I can be of any further help to you, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [Signature]
                                        Janis Shank McLean
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

JSM:pln

sections.question: if the MVMA provides certain Board members and staff with the trip to Detroit, who will be considered to be the source of this gift for the purposes of the Act's disclosure and disqualification requirements.
sections.conclusion: Each Board and staff member receiving the trip must report his or her share of the gift from the MVMA in his or her Statement of Economic Interests. Under the staff's current policy, the source of this income will be considered to be MVMA, not the individual members of MVMA. However, the staff feels that, in a situation such as you have presented, where an association has only a few members who may be materially affected by governmental decision making, there may be justification for treating both the association and the members as sources of income.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether gifts provided by a trade association (MVMA) to Air Resources Board members and staff constitute a financial interest requiring disclosure and disqualification, and who should be identified as the source of the gift.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the MVMA is the source of the gift for disclosure purposes, and each recipient must report it on their Statement of Economic Interests. However, the FPPC acknowledges that where an association has few members materially affected by governmental decisions, both the association and its members may warrant treatment as income sources, and the FPPC plans to explore a regulation on this issue.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC advice letter from 1984 with clear question and conclusion sections. Some OCR errors present in original ('pUblic' instead of 'public', 'wfinancial' instead of 'financial', etc.) but content is clear. Statutory references and policy discussion are comprehensive.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: if the MVMA provides certain Board members and staff with the trip to Detroit, who will be considered to be the source of this gift for the purposes of the Act's disclosure and disqualification requirements.

CONCLUSION: Each Board and staff member receiving the trip must report his or her share of the gift from the MVMA in his or her Statement of Economic Interests. Under the staff's current policy, the source of this income will be considered to be MVMA, not the individual members of MVMA. However, the staff feels that, in a situation such as you have presented, where an association has only a few members who may be materially affected by governmental decision making, there may be justification for treating both the association and the members as sources of income.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the Air Resources Board that gifts from the Motor Vehicle Manufacturers Association exceeding $250 must be reported by recipients on their Statements of Economic Interests, with MVMA identified as the source under current policy, though the FPPC indicates it may reconsider this approach for trade associations with few members affected by the agency's decisions.
